name: Clean and Press
description: A full-body compound movement. Pull the barbell from the floor to shoulders in one explosive drive, then press overhead. Builds total-body coordination and strength.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the barbell just outside your legs. Hinge at the hips, back flat, chest up.
Drive through your legs and hips explosively. Pull the bar to shoulder height and rotate your elbows forward into the front rack position.
Press the bar directly overhead until your arms fully lock out. Lower with control back to the front rack, then to the floor.

name: Barbell Squat
description: The foundational lower-body exercise. The barbell rests across your upper back as you descend under control into a full squat. Builds leg and core strength.
steps: Position the bar across your upper back (not your neck). Grip the bar firmly, feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out.
Descend by simultaneously bending your knees and hips. Keep your chest tall and your knees tracking over your toes.
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor. Drive through your heels to return to the starting position.

name: Pullover
description: A classic chest and back exercise performed lying on a bench. Opens the rib cage through a long arc of motion. Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout.
steps: Lie flat on a bench, feet on the floor. Hold the barbell with both hands at arm's length directly above your chest.
Keeping your arms slightly bent, slowly lower the bar back over your head in a controlled sweeping arc.
Lower until you feel a full stretch in your chest and lats. Reverse the arc and return the bar to the starting position above your chest.

name: Bench Press
description: The classic chest pressing movement. Lie flat and press the barbell from your lower chest to full arm extension. Focus on control both up and down.
steps: Lie flat on the bench with your feet planted on the floor. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width with a firm overhand grip.
Unrack the bar and lower it under control until it touches your lower chest. Keep your elbows at roughly 45° from your body.
Press the bar explosively back to full arm extension. Do not lock out aggressively. Lower with control and repeat.

name: Good Morning
description: A hip hinge exercise with the bar on your upper back. Strengthens the posterior chain — hamstrings, glutes, and lower back. Use light weight and perfect form.
steps: Stand upright with the bar resting on your upper back. Feet shoulder-width apart. Soft bend in your knees. Brace your core.
Keeping your back perfectly flat, push your hips back and bend forward at the hips. Allow only a slight additional bend in the knees.
Lower your torso until it is roughly parallel to the floor — or as far as your hamstring flexibility allows. Drive your hips forward to return to standing.

name: Barbell Curl
description: The standard bicep curl with a straight barbell. Keep your elbows pinned at your sides throughout the entire movement. No swinging.
steps: Stand upright holding the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary at your sides, curl the bar upward by contracting your biceps.
Curl until the bar reaches shoulder height and your biceps are fully contracted. Squeeze at the top, then lower slowly back to full extension.

name: Wrist Curl
description: A forearm isolation exercise performed seated. Builds grip strength and forearm size. Bruce Lee placed exceptional emphasis on forearm development.
steps: Sit on a bench with your forearms resting on your thighs, wrists hanging just beyond your knees. Hold the bar with a palms-up (supinated) grip.
Allow your wrists to extend and lower toward the floor as far as comfortable. The bar may roll down to your fingertips slightly.
Curl your wrists upward as far as possible, squeezing your forearms hard at the top. Lower slowly and repeat.

name: Reverse Curl
description: The barbell curl with an overhand grip. Targets the brachialis and forearm extensors — muscles often neglected with standard curls.
steps: Stand upright holding the barbell with an overhand (pronated) grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Arms fully extended.
Keeping your elbows at your sides, curl the bar upward. The overhand grip makes this significantly harder than a standard curl.
Curl until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or slightly higher. Lower under control back to full extension.
